Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE1: Search emails for the Patricia Kwon tip using search_emails (query: "Patricia Kwon" or "settlement fee" or "Fidelity National Title"). Discovery: Email from patricia.kwon@fnf.com to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com with subject "Settlement fee patterns on recent Keystone files", a vague tip about unusual settlement charges on recent Keystone closings, suggests comparing March fee breakdowns between settlement providers. Does NOT name a specific vendor or LO.
OE2: Search emails for Robert's vendor review request using search_emails (query: "vendor cost review" or "Q2"). Discovery: Email from robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com requesting the Q2 vendor cost overview for the board compliance summary, due by the end of the day Friday, focused on settlement and title vendors.
OE3: Search emails for Denise-Grace correspondence about the review using search_emails (query: "settlement fee review" or "Piedmont" or "Grace"). Discovery: Denise's email to Grace asking her to pull March closing files from Piedmont vs Tryon for fee comparison, mentions the outside tip aligning with Grace's earlier flag, and requests confidentiality.
OE4: Search QuickBooks bills for Piedmont Settlement Services using quickbooks_search_bills (query: "Piedmont" or "V-PIEDMONT-SETTLE"). Discovery: Six recent Piedmont bills (BILL-TTL-sc7a-001 through 006) for March closings (LN-2026-00701 through 00706), each containing TWO line items: (1) normal settlement services fee ($265-$340) and (2) a suspicious "Recording coordination fee" ($185-$225). Total per bill ranges from $450-$565. The recording coordination fee totals $1,230 across all six files.
OE5: Search QuickBooks bills for Tryon Title using quickbooks_search_bills (query: "Tryon" or "V-TRYON-TITLE"). Discovery: Four recent Tryon Title bills for comparable March closings, each containing only ONE line item (settlement services, $275-$330). NO recording coordination fee line appears on any Tryon bill, confirming the fee is unique to Piedmont.
OE6: Search Stripe charges for recording fees on the affected loans using stripe_search_charges or stripe_list_charges (query: "recording fee" or loan numbers from the Piedmont files). Discovery: Six county recording fee charges to borrowers on the same six loans, ranging from $125-$150 each. This means borrowers paid recording fees at closing AND Piedmont separately billed Keystone $185-$225 per file for "recording coordination", creating a double-billing pattern where both borrower and broker are paying for what appears to be the same service.
OE7: Search mortgage LOS for the affected loan files using mortgage_los_search_loans (query for loan numbers LN-2026-00701 through 00706 or title_company "Piedmont"). Discovery: Six closed loans, all with title_company "Piedmont Settlement Services." Five (LN-2026-00701, 00702, 00703, 00704, 00706) are assigned to James Thornton (los_staff_ef0febeeff53). One (LN-2026-00705) is assigned to Derek Moss (los_staff_f9aa4c3c2fcb). This reveals disproportionate LO routing: Thornton originated 5 of 6 Piedmont files.
OE8: Search Slack for discussions about settlement vendors or Piedmont using conversations_search_messages (query: "Piedmont" or "settlement" or "recording coordination"). Discovery: (1) James Thornton's message in #loan-processing (C002) on April 10 casually recommending Piedmont to other LOs: "I always route to Piedmont, their turnaround is fastest." (2) Grace's message in #loan-processing on April 8 noting Derek's Whitfield file (LN-2026-00705) was reassigned from Thornton because James was out that week. This explains why the one Derek Moss file exists (it was originally Thornton's file, reassigned during his PTO). This is a critical context: all 6 files trace back to Thornton's pipeline/influence, not a split between two LOs.
OE9: Search Slack DMs for Grace-Denise discussion about fees using conversations_search_messages (query: "reconciling" or "Piedmont" or "recording coordination" in D_denise_grace). Discovery: (1) Grace's April 15 DM to Denise flagging Piedmont's recording coordination line and suggesting a side-by-side comparison with Tryon. (2) Denise's reply asking Grace to pull the comparison and check if this is a new fee pattern. (3) Denise's April 25 DM escalating the review to formal status, mentioning the outside tip confirmation and flagging potential RESPA Section 8 concern if the fee maps to one LO.
OE10: Search CRM engagements for notes about Piedmont, settlement fees, or vendor review using crm_list_engagements (query: "Piedmont" or "settlement fee" or "vendor review"). Discovery: (1) Grace's April 14 note flagging Piedmont's recording coordination fee discrepancy vs Tryon, noting six March bills all carry the fee. (2) Denise's April 16 note opening a preliminary compliance review of Piedmont billing practices, which contains UNIQUE critical information not available in any other service: Elena Marchetti reported that Thornton specifically asked Grace to keep the Whitfield file with Piedmont during his PTO (rather than letting Derek choose a vendor), and that Elena overheard Thornton reference a personal contact at Piedmont by first name. This is the strongest evidence of a potential personal financial relationship between Thornton and the vendor. (3) Robert's April 21 vendor review request note, whose timing aligns with the Piedmont investigation.
OE11: Email Robert Calloway (robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com or r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com) with settlement fee findings using send_email. Content should summarize: Piedmont's undisclosed recording coordination fee ($185-$225 per file, $1,230 total across 6 files), borrowers also charged recording fees via Stripe, one LO (Thornton) routing disproportionately to Piedmont, potential RESPA Section 8 violation, and recommended next steps.
OE12: Email Grace Yamamoto (grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com) with operational details using send_email. Content should identify the six affected loan files (LN-2026-00701 through 00706), flag that new Piedmont file assignments should be held, note that LN-2026-00705 was originally Thornton's file reassigned to Derek, and outline what Grace needs to pull for the deeper review.
OE13: Write a formal compliance risk memo to the filesystem (via filesystem_write_file, e.g. to compliance/settlement_vendor_fee_memo.txt or similar path) documenting the vendor fee analysis: the Piedmont recording coordination fee pattern ($185-$225/file, $1,230 total), the comparison with Tryon (no such fee), the LO routing concentration (Thornton on 5 of 6 files), the borrower double-billing via Stripe, and the RESPA Section 8 regulatory assessment. This serves as board-review backup documentation.
OE14: Post in #compliance-alerts Slack channel (C004) using conversations_add_message. Content should announce that a settlement vendor fee audit is underway and instruct the team to hold any new file assignments to the affected vendor until further notice.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE5: Search QuickBooks bills for Tryon Title using quickbooks_search_bills (query: "Tryon" or "V-TRYON-TITLE"). This means borrowers paid recording fees at closing AND Piedmont separately billed Keystone $185-$225 per file for "recording coordination", creating a double-billing pattern where both borrower and broker are paying for what appears to be the same service.

query construction

✓Run #4The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with criteria targeting 'V-PIEDMONT-SETTLE' (containing 'Piedmont') and 'V-TRYON-TITLE', and subsequently called it with settlement-related vendor names discovered from prior research. The query directly targeted the Piedmont vendor.
✓Run #5The agent called quickbooks_search_vendors with criteria filtering on DisplayName LIKE '%title%' and '%settlement%', both of which are relevant queries that surface the settlement vendors.
